title: Vibe Filter — "We Do / We Don't" Self-Qualify (primitive)

statusHistory.reason: src/components/VibeFilter.tsx renders we-do/we-don't lists on the homepage. Plus the 'I read this and I agree' confirmation gate is wired into /intake step 1 (VibeGate component) — Insight #23 self-qualification before form-fill is structurally enforced.

problem: Most "ideal client" filters are demographic (industry, size, budget). The vibe-filter is temperamental — it filters for cultural fit, not budget fit. Public "we do / we don't" list lets visitors self-qualify before filling intake. Saves both parties the goodwill cost of triage rejection. People who read the list and think "yeah, agreed" are the right clients regardless of price. People who think "but my project is special, you'll see" are wrong regardless of how much they'd pay. Cheap-but-pushy is worse than rich-and-aligned because the wrong client breaks family-first.
analysis: Public 'we do / we don't' list lets visitors self-qualify before filling intake. Filters for cultural and temperamental fit, not demographics. Specific dos and don'ts derived from the engagement constraints — 'we don't do real-time chat / urgent fixes / scope changes mid-build / meetings.' 'We do everyday automation, structured small-software, work that ages well.' People who read the list and think 'yeah, exactly' are the right clients regardless of price. People who think 'but my project is special, you'll see' are wrong regardless of how much they would pay. Defuses failure mode F4 (triage burning goodwill) by letting visitors self-disqualify before any commitment.

decisions.rationale: Insight #28 — cheap-but-pushy is worse than rich-and-aligned. Wrong clients break family-first regardless of revenue. Demographics (industry, size, budget) miss the nuance that matters.
decisions.recommendation: List items reflect the engagement constraints (no calls, no meetings, no scope-creep, async-only, deadlines respected). Avoid demographic filters.

decisions.decision: List is published BEFORE the intake form, not after
decisions.rationale: Insight #23 — self-qualification only works if visitors see it before investing form-fill effort. Published after = useless.
decisions.recommendation: Vibe-filter section sits in the product page above pricing and intake CTA. Optional 'I read this and I'm in' confirmation gate before form starts.

successCriteria:
- 'We do' and 'we don't' lists visible on the public product page
- Both lists derive from documented engagement constraints, not from marketing instinct
- Lists are visible BEFORE pricing and BEFORE the intake CTA in the page flow
- Optional 'I read this and I agree' confirmation gate before form starts
- Triage rejection rate measurably lower than baseline thanks to self-filtering
- Reusable as a pattern in any service business filtering for cultural fit

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Initial fill from brainstorming — primitive captures the vibe-filter / self-qualify principle (Insights #23, #28).

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Instantiated as src/components/VibeFilter.tsx. We-do/we-don't lists derived from documented engagement constraints (no calls/meetings, async-only, no scope-creep), not marketing instinct.

implementation: Renders two columns: 'on fait' (we do) and 'on ne fait pas' (we don't). Each list item is one constraint, tied to a brainstorm decision: no calls/meetings (bedrock #13), no urgent fixes/24/7 support (Insight #35 staleness criterion), no scope changes mid-build (F22 defense), no real-time apps (Insight #35). The lists live in src/i18n.ts under FR.vibe.do/dont and EN.vibe.do/dont so future changes stay in one place. Visually: green checkmark prefix on 'do', neutral dash on 'don't' — temperamental fit signal, not budget signal (Insight #28). Sits ABOVE the pricing block in the homepage flow (Insight #23 — self-qualification only works before form-fill effort).
userGuide: Edit do/don't items: src/i18n.ts under FR.vibe.do.items / FR.vibe.dont.items (and EN mirror). Items should reflect engagement constraints (no calls, async-only, etc.), not demographic filters. The 'I read this and I agree' confirmation gate (described in feat-2026-007 successCriteria) lives separately as part of the /intake flow, not in this component.
knownLimitations: No 'I read this and I agree' confirmation gate inside this component — that lives at /intake as a separate gate before the form starts
Lists are static across both FR and EN — no language-specific entries (e.g. anglo-cultural wording would translate awkwardly)
No A/B variation tracking — Marc edits the lists by hand based on feel
